elements.sent-2411-001: Using the properties of generalized Fibonacci numbers, we determine the automorphism groups of some K3 surfaces with Picard number $2$.
elements.sent-2411-002: Conversely, using the automorphisms of K3 surfaces with Picard number $2$, we prove the criterion for a given integer $n$ is to be a generalized Fibonacci number.
elements.sent-2411-003: Moreover, we show that the generalized $k$-th Fibonacci number divides the generalized $q$-th Fibonacci number if and only if $k$ divides $q$.
elements.sent-2411-004: A K3 surface is a simply connected compact manifold $X$ such that $H^0(X,\Omega_X^2)=\mathbb{C}\omega_X$, where $\omega_X$ is an everywhere non-degenerate holomorphic $2$-form on $X$.
elements.sent-2411-006: Oguiso considered a K3 surface $S$ with Picard lattice $NS(S)=\Z h_1\oplus \Z h_2$ where the intersection matrix \begin{equation}\label{Picard lattice of Oguiso} [(h_i,h_j)]=\begin{bmatrix} 4 2 2 -4\end{bmatrix}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-007: He constructed an isometry of $NS(S)$ given by the multiplication of $\eta^6$ on $NS(S)$, where $h_2=\eta:=\frac{1+\sqrt{5 {2}$ is a basis of the lattice $NS(S)$ together with $h_1:=1$.}}
elements.sent-2411-008: Then by the global Torelli theorem for K3 surfaces, it induces an automorphism $g$ of $S$ with positive entropy without fixed points.
elements.sent-2411-009: It is also known as the Cayley-Oguiso automorphism in \cite{FGvGvL .}

elements.sent-2411-038: For two polynomials $P, Q\in \Z[x]$ without common zeros in $\C$, the resultant of $P$ and $Q$ is the integer given by \begin{equation}\label{resultant} \res\{P, Q\}=\prod_{P(u)=0,Q(v)=0}(u-v).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-039: We use the fact that $\bar{P (x),\bar{Q (x)$ have common factors in $\F_p[x]$ if and only if $p\mid \res\{P, Q\}$, where $P\mapsto \bar{P}$ under $\Z[x]\rightarrow \F_p[x]$.}}
elements.sent-2411-040: A lattice is a free $\Z$-module $L$ of finite rank equipped with a symmetric bilinear form $\langle ~,~ \rangle \colon L\times L\rightarrow \Z$.
elements.sent-2411-041: If $x^2:=\langle x,x\rangle \in 2\Z$ for any $x\in L$, a lattice $L$ is said to be even.
elements.sent-2411-042: We fix a $\Z$-basis of $L$ and identify the lattice $L$ with its intersection matrix $Q_L$ under this basis.
elements.sent-2411-043: The discriminant $\disc(L)$ of $L$ is defined as $\det(Q_L)$, which is independent of the choice of basis.
elements.sent-2411-044: A lattice $L$ is called non-degenerate if $\disc(L)\neq 0$ and unimodular if $\disc(L)=\pm1$.
elements.sent-2411-045: For a non-degenerate lattice $L$, the signature of $L$ is defined as $(s_+,s_-)$, where $s_{+}$ (resp.\ $s_-$) denotes the number of the positive (resp.\ negative) eigenvalues of $Q_L$.
elements.sent-2411-046: An isometry of $L$ is an automorphism of the $\Z$-module $L$ preserving the bilinear form.
elements.sent-2411-047: The orthogonal group $O(L)$ of $L$ consists of the isometries of $L$ and we have the following identification: \begin{equation}\label{EQ_ortho} O(L)= \{ g\in GL_n(\Z) \bigm| g^T \cdot Q_L \cdot g=Q_L \}, \quad n=\rank L.\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-009: \begin{equation}\label{EQ_ortho} O(L)= \{ g\in GL_n(\Z) \bigm| g^T \cdot Q_L \cdot g=Q_L \}, \quad n=\rank L.\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-048: For a non-degenerate lattice $L$, the discriminant group $A(L)$ of $L$ is defined by \begin{equation}A(L):=L^*/L, \quad L^*:= \{ x\in L\otimes \Q \bigm| \langle x,y \rangle \in \Z ~ \forall y\in L \}.\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-010: \begin{equation}A(L):=L^*/L, \quad L^*:= \{ x\in L\otimes \Q \bigm| \langle x,y \rangle \in \Z ~ \forall y\in L \}.\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-049: Let $K$ be a sublattice of a lattice $L$, that is, $K$ is a $\Z$-submodule of $L$ equipped with the restriction of the bilinear form of $L$ to $K$.
elements.sent-2411-050: If $L/K$ is torsion-free as a $\Z$-module, $K$ is said to be primitive.
elements.sent-2411-051: For a non-degenerate lattice $L$ of signature $(1,k)$ with $k\geq 1$, we have the decomposition \begin{equation}\label{EQ_positive_cone} \{x\in L\otimes \R \bigm| x^2 >0 \}=C_L\sqcup (-C_L)\end{equation} into two disjoint cones.
elements.sent-2411-052: Here $C_L$ and $-C_L$ are connected components and $C_L$ is called the positive cone.
elements.sent-2411-053: We define \begin{equation}\label{O^+} O^+(L):=\{g\in O(L) \bigm| g(C_L)=C_L \}, \quad SO^+(L):=O^+(L)\cap SO(L),\end{equation} where $SO(L)$ is the subgroup of $O(L)$ consisting of isometries of determinant $1$.
elements.sent-2411-054: The group $O^+(L)$ is a subgroup of $O(L)$ of index $2$.
elements.sent-2411-055: For $g\in O(L)$ and $\epsilon\in\{\pm 1\}$, $g$ acts on $A(L)$ as $\epsilon\cdot \id$ if and only if $(g-\epsilon\cdot I_n)\cdot Q_L^{-1}$ is an integer matrix.

elements.sent-2411-058: Since $X$ has no $(-2)$-curve, the ample cone of $X$ is just the positive cone, i.e., \begin{equation}\label{ample cone of X} \begin{array}{ll} \Amp(X) =\{(x,y)\in NS(X)\otimes \R\mid (x,y)^2>0 \text{ and } x>0\} =\{(x,y)\in NS(X)\otimes \R\mid x>0 \text{ and }\frac{a-\sqrt{a^2+4}}{2}<\frac{y}{x}<\frac{a+\sqrt{a^2+4}}{2}\}. \end{array}\end{equation} Let $g$ be an automorphism of a K3 surface $X$.

elements.sent-2411-063: A Salem trace is an algebraic integer $\tau>2$ whose other conjugates lie in $(-2,2)$; the minimal polynomial of such a number is called a Salem trace polynomial.
